title: ganymedes capax
side_effects: Ganymedes capax can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting, which can be triggered by excessive consumption or contamination. In some cases, long-term misuse can lead to liver damage, anemia, and even organ failure. Additionally, improper preparation or dosing of the plant can cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues. Furthermore, Ganymedes capax can interact with other medications, exacerbating symptoms of existing health conditions or causing new ones to arise.
